title: Instant Category Frequency Counts with collections.Counter
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Native Frequency Counter & Top K Items
from collections import Counter
counts = Counter(categories)
top_3 = counts.most_common(3)
hinglish_explanation: Raw list me values ki frequency (kitni baar konsi city/category aayi) count karne ke liye loop ki jagah `Counter(list)` use karo. `most_common(3)` top 3 categories instant nikal deta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain Python collections.Counter for exploratory data analysis (EDA) on text streams.

title: Group Categorical Records with collections.defaultdict
wizard_code:
# Auto-Grouping Default Dictionary
from collections import defaultdict
groups = defaultdict(list)
for dept, emp in emp_data:
    groups[dept].append(emp)
hinglish_explanation: Data ko category ke basis par list me group karne ke liye `if key not in dict` condition likhne ki zarurat nahi. `defaultdict(list)` missing keys ko automatic empty list me initialize karta hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate Python defaultdict(list) for grouping JSON dataset payloads by ID.

title: Fast Sliding Window Buffer with collections.deque
naive_code:
# Slow O(N) list pop from left for moving window
window = [1, 2, 3]
window.pop(0)  # Re-indexes entire list in RAM
window.append(4)
wizard_code:
# O(1) Fixed-Size Sliding Buffer
from collections import deque
window = deque(maxlen=3)  # Automatically drops oldest item on append
for val in data_stream:
    window.append(val)
hinglish_explanation: Streaming data (e.g. live stock prices) me last 30 items ka sliding buffer rakhne ke liye `deque(maxlen=30)` best tool hai. Purana item automatic drop ho jata hai bina manual list pop ke!
ai_prompt: Show how collections.deque(maxlen=N) calculates moving averages in streaming data.

title: Pair Parallel Lists into Mapping Dict via dict(zip())
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Zip Dictionary Mapper
mapping = dict(zip(user_ids, names))
hinglish_explanation: Do parallel lists (e.g. IDs aur Names) ko key-value lookup dict banane ke liye loop ki jagah `dict(zip(ids, names))` 1 line me mapping dict taiyar karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show how dict(zip(keys, values)) is used to create column replacement maps in data analytics.

title: Flatten Nested Lists from API Responses via itertools.chain
wizard_code:
# C-Speed Iterator List Flattening
from itertools import chain
flat_tags = list(chain.from_iterable(user_tags))
hinglish_explanation: JSON APIs se aane wale nested lists (`[[tag1, tag2], [tag3]]`) ko single flat list me combine karne ke liye `chain.from_iterable()` C-speed iterator engine use karta hai!
ai_prompt: Compare itertools.chain.from_iterable vs nested list comprehensions for data flattening.

title: Transpose Row Matrix to Columns with zip(*matrix)
naive_code:
# Nested loops to swap rows and columns in 2D data list
transposed = []
for c in range(len(rows[0])):
    transposed.append([rows[r][c] for r in range(len(rows))])
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Pythonic Row-to-Column Swap
columns = [list(col) for col in zip(*rows)]
hinglish_explanation: 2D Data rows ko columns me invert (transpose) karne ke liye `zip(*rows)` unpacking sabse clean aur fast shortcut hai!
ai_prompt: Show how zip(*matrix) unpacks rows into column tuples in pure Python.

title: Top K Highest Scoring Items via heapq.nlargest
naive_code:
# Full sort O(N log N) to extract top 3 customers
top_3 = sorted(customers, key=lambda c: c['spend'], reverse=True)[:3]
wizard_code:
# Min-Heap O(N log K) Top Extractor
import heapq
top_3 = heapq.nlargest(3, customers, key=lambda c: c['spend'])
hinglish_explanation: Millions of customer records me se top 3 highest spenders nikalne ke liye pure list ko `sorted()` mat karo. `heapq.nlargest(3, data, key=...)` min-heap algorithm se fast slice karta hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate heapq.nlargest vs heapq.nsmallest for analytical top-tier ranking.

title: Merge Dictionaries with Union Operator |
naive_code:
# Merging config dicts using copy and update
final_config = default_config.copy()
final_config.update(user_config)
wizard_code:
# Clean Pipe Union Operator (Python 3.9+)
final_config = default_config | user_config
hinglish_explanation: Python 3.9+ me do metadata ya configuration dictionaries ko merge karne ke liye `.update()` ki jagah direct `dict1 | dict2` pipe operator write karo!
ai_prompt: Explain Python 3.9+ dictionary union operator | and inplace union |=.

title: Extract Head, Tail & Middle via Extended Unpacking (*rest)
naive_code:
# Index slicing to separate first entry, last entry, and middle data
first_val = scores[0]
last_val = scores[-1]
middle_vals = scores[1:-1]
wizard_code:
# Elegant Sequence Unpacking
first_val, *middle_vals, last_val = scores
hinglish_explanation: Ordered scores ya time-series list se starting, ending aur middle values unpack karne ke liye `first, *middle, last = list` intuitive syntax hai!
ai_prompt: Show extended star unpacking in function return tuples and list slicing.

title: Self-Documenting Debug Output via f'{val=}'
naive_code:
# Manual variable name printing during data inspection
print('total_sales =', total_sales, 'count =', count)
wizard_code:
# Self-Logging Debug Syntax
print(f'{total_sales=}, {count=}')
hinglish_explanation: Data cleaning me variables inspect karte waqt `print(f'{total_sales=}')` variable name aur uski computed value automatic formatting ke saath print kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Show F-String debug syntax f'{var=}' with number formatting specifiers.

title: Inline Assignment with Walrus Operator (:=)
naive_code:
# Evaluating regex or search twice in condition and body
match = pattern.search(text)
if match:
    process(match.group(1))
hinglish_explanation: Data extraction me search evaluation dubara na likhna pade, `:=` walrus operator condition check ke saath variable assign kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate walrus operator := in list comprehensions for filtering non-null clean values.

title: Fast Set Intersection & Union for Customer ID Overlaps
naive_code:
# Slow nested loop to find common active users between two campaigns
common_users = [u for u in campaign_a if u in campaign_b]
wizard_code:
# O(N) Hash Set Intersection
common_users = list(set(campaign_a) & set(campaign_b))
hinglish_explanation: Do customer ID lists me common active users dhundne ke liye nested loop O(N^2) mat chalao. Set intersection `&` O(N) hash speed me kaam karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show set operations & (intersection), | (union), and - (difference) for customer cohort overlaps.

title: Index-Aware Iteration via enumerate(start=1)
naive_code:
# Manual row index counter variable
i = 1
for row in data:
    print(f'Row {i}: {row}')
    i += 1
hinglish_explanation: Rows print karte waqt index track karne ke liye manual `i += 1` variable set mat karo. `enumerate(data, start=1)` clean generator indexing offer karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show enumerate() in list comprehensions and dictionary generators.

title: Cache Expensive Data Cleaning Functions via @lru_cache
hinglish_explanation: Repeated city names ya text strings ke liye heavy geocoding API / parsing calculation ko `@lru_cache` se cache karlo! Performance 100x fast ho jayegi!
ai_prompt: Explain functools.lru_cache for caching API lookup calls in data pipelines.

title: Fast Key-Based Sorting via operator.itemgetter
naive_code:
# Slow Python lambda key sorting for list of dicts
sorted_data = sorted(records, key=lambda x: x['score'], reverse=True)
wizard_code:
# C-Speed Itemgetter Key Extraction
from operator import itemgetter
sorted_data = sorted(records, key=itemgetter('score'), reverse=True)
hinglish_explanation: List of dictionaries ko key ('score') par sort karne ke liye lambda ki jagah `itemgetter('score')` C-level field extraction use karta hai jo 30% fast hai!
ai_prompt: Compare operator.itemgetter vs lambda key functions in list sorting.

title: Short-Circuit Data Checks via any() and all()
wizard_code:
# Generator Short-Circuit Evaluator
has_invalid = any(row is None for row in data)
hinglish_explanation: `any()` aur `all()` generator expressions first match milte hi iteration stop kar dete hain (short-circuiting), making data validation super clean!
ai_prompt: Show any() and all() for data validation checks across dataset rows.

title: Gracefully Ignore Missing File Errors via contextlib.suppress
wizard_code:
# Clean Suppress Context Manager
from contextlib import suppress
with suppress(FileNotFoundError):
    os.remove('temp_cache.csv')
hinglish_explanation: Harmless file missing error ko ignore karne ke liye ugly try-except-pass block ki jagah `with suppress(FileNotFoundError):` write karo!
ai_prompt: Show contextlib.suppress for ignoring harmless errors in data pipeline scripts.

title: Lazy Generator Expressions vs Memory Heavy Lists
naive_code:
# Loads 10 Million items into RAM at once (~800MB RAM)
total_sum = sum([x**2 for x in range(10_000_000)])
wizard_code:
# Streams 1 item at a time in O(1) Memory (~100 Bytes)
total_sum = sum(x**2 for x in range(10_000_000))
hinglish_explanation: Square brackets `[...]` pure list ko RAM me load kar dete hain. Parentheses `(...)` generator lazy stream karta hai jisse RAM usage ~100 bytes reh jati hai!
ai_prompt: Explain lazy evaluation in Python generators vs list comprehensions with memory sizing.

title: Instant O(1) Lookups using Sets vs List Scans
naive_code:
# O(N) linear search inside a list of valid IDs
is_valid = user_id in valid_id_list  # Scans elements one by one
wizard_code:
# O(1) Instant Hash Lookup
valid_id_set = set(valid_id_list)
is_valid = user_id in valid_id_set  # Instant hash lookup
hinglish_explanation: Large lists me `in` operator O(N) time leta hai. List ko `set` me convert karne se hash table lookup O(1) constant time ban jata hai!
ai_prompt: Show benchmark comparison of list in search vs set in search across 100,000 customer IDs.

title: Fast String List Aggregation with str.join()
naive_code:
# Slow O(N^2) string concatenation loop
full_text = ''
for word in word_list:
    full_text += word + ' '
wizard_code:
# Fast O(N) Single-Pass String Join
full_text = ' '.join(word_list)
hinglish_explanation: Loop me `+=` se strings append karne par har baar naya memory allocation O(N^2) hota hai. `' '.join(word_list)` 1-pass O(N) execution deta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain string immutability in Python and why str.join optimizes memory buffers.

title: Check Object RAM Footprint in Bytes via sys.getsizeof
naive_code:
# Guessing memory size of large data structure
print(len(my_data_dict))
wizard_code:
# Exact Memory Inspection in Bytes
import sys
size_bytes = sys.getsizeof(my_data_dict)
hinglish_explanation: `sys.getsizeof(object)` kisi bhi list, dictionary ya dataset ka exact byte-level memory size reveal karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show how sys.getsizeof is used to compare memory footprints of data structures.

title: Safe Data Evaluation with ast.literal_eval
naive_code:
# Dangerous arbitrary code execution using eval()
data = eval(raw_string_from_csv)
wizard_code:
# Safe Literal Structure Parser
import ast
data = ast.literal_eval(raw_string_from_csv)
hinglish_explanation: CSV/JSON se stringified dicts ya lists (`"{'a': 1}"`) convert karne ke liye `eval()` security risk hai. `ast.literal_eval()` safely parse karta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain security risks of eval() and why ast.literal_eval() is safe for parsing stringified data.

title: Accurate Benchmarking with time.perf_counter()
naive_code:
# System clock affected by NTP sync
import time
t0 = time.time()
run_pipeline()
print(time.time() - t0)
wizard_code:
# Monotonic High-Precision Performance Timer
import time
t0 = time.perf_counter()
run_pipeline()
print(f'Elapsed: {time.perf_counter() - t0:.6f}s')
hinglish_explanation: Code performance benchmark karne ke liye `time.time()` system clock shift ho sakta hai. `time.perf_counter()` monotonic hardware timer use karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show time.perf_counter() inside a Python context manager to measure execution time.

title: Invert Lookup Dictionaries via Dict Comprehensions
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Dict Comprehension Inversion
inverted = {v: k for k, v in original_map.items()}
hinglish_explanation: Mapping dictionary ko reverse lookup dictionary me invert karne ke liye `{v: k for k, v in original.items()}` 1-liner use karo!
ai_prompt: Show dictionary comprehensions with conditional filtering for data mapping.

title: Check Multiple Prefixes with str.startswith(tuple)
hinglish_explanation: `startswith()` me tuple `('data', 'raw', 'staging')` pass kar sakte ho! Extra `OR` conditions likhne ki zarurat nahi hoti!
ai_prompt: Show str.startswith and str.endswith using tuples for file name filtering.

title: Split Strings safely into 3 Parts with str.partition()
naive_code:
# Splitting email by delimiter using index search and slicing
idx = email.find('@')
username = email[:idx]
domain = email[idx+1:]
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Tuple Partitioning
username, sep, domain = email.partition('@')
hinglish_explanation: `email.partition('@')` string ko exactly 3 parts `(head, sep, tail)` me split karta hai. `find()` aur manual slice math ka jhanjhat khatam!
ai_prompt: Show str.partition() vs str.split() for parsing key-value string pairs.

title: Unicode-Safe Case Matching with str.casefold()
naive_code:
# Standard str.lower() fails on international text accents
if text1.lower() == text2.lower(): ...
wizard_code:
# Unicode-Compliant Casefolded Matching
if text1.casefold() == text2.casefold(): ...
hinglish_explanation: International text matching me `str.lower()` har character convert nahi karta. `casefold()` aggressive Unicode caseless comparison execute karta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain str.casefold() vs str.lower() for international text normalization.

title: Clean Prefix Removal via str.removeprefix()
naive_code:
# Risky str.lstrip() which strips individual characters
clean_url = raw_url.lstrip('https://')
wizard_code:
# Exact Prefix Trimmer (Python 3.9+)
clean_url = raw_url.removeprefix('https://')
hinglish_explanation: `lstrip('https://')` URL me se 'h', 't', 'p', 's' matching chars strip kar deta hai! `removeprefix()` exact string match trim karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show difference between str.lstrip() and str.removeprefix() with safety examples.

title: Clean Extension Trimming via str.removesuffix()
wizard_code:
# Native Extension Trimmer (Python 3.9+)
base_name = filename.removesuffix('.csv')
hinglish_explanation: File extensions trim karne ke liye index length math (`[:-4]`) mat karo. `removesuffix('.csv')` safe 1-liner extension stripper hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate str.removesuffix() vs os.path.splitext() for file name cleaning.

title: Fast Bulk Character Cleaning with str.translate()
naive_code:
# Chained str.replace() calls in a loop
clean_text = raw_text.replace('!', '').replace('?', '').replace('#', '')
wizard_code:
# Single-Pass C-Speed Character Mapping
trans = str.maketrans('', '', '!?#$')
clean_text = raw_text.translate(trans)
hinglish_explanation: Multiple punctuation marks strip karne ke liye multiple `replace()` chained calls memory bloat karti hain. `translate()` 1-pass C speed me chars remove karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show how str.maketrans and str.translate sanitize text data 10x faster than multiple replace() calls.

title: Zero-Padded Numeric IDs via str.zfill()
naive_code:
# String concatenation to pad zeroes for user IDs
id_str = '0' * (6 - len(str(user_id))) + str(user_id)
wizard_code:
# Native Zero Padding
id_str = str(user_id).zfill(6)  # Output: '000042'
hinglish_explanation: Customer IDs (e.g. `000042`) me leading zeroes append karne ke liye length math ki jagah `.zfill(6)` call karo!
ai_prompt: Show str.zfill() vs F-String format specifiers like f'{num:06d}'.

title: Sort Mixed Case Strings via sorted(key=str.lower)
naive_code:
# Default ASCII sorting puts uppercase 'Z' before lowercase 'a'
sorted_cities = sorted(city_list) # ['Zebra', 'apple', 'banana']
wizard_code:
# Case-Insensitive Alphabetical Sorting
sorted_cities = sorted(city_list, key=str.lower) # ['apple', 'banana', 'Zebra']
hinglish_explanation: Default ASCII `sorted()` uppercase letters ko lowercase se pehle rakhta hai. `key=str.lower` true alphabetical order ensure karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show how sorted(key=str.lower) handles mixed-case string lists.

title: Find Max Record by Specific Dict Key via max(key=...)
naive_code:
# Sorting full list just to get highest revenue record
top_order = sorted(orders, key=lambda o: o['amount'], reverse=True)[0]
wizard_code:
# Direct O(N) Linear Max Record Extraction
top_order = max(orders, key=lambda o: o['amount'])
hinglish_explanation: Pura dataset sort O(N log N) karne ki jagah `max(orders, key=lambda x: x['amount'])` O(N) linear time me top record extract kar leta hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate max() and min() with key parameter on list of dictionaries.

title: Combine Unequal Lists with itertools.zip_longest
naive_code:
# Manual loop padding shorter list with N/A default
max_len = max(len(a), len(b))
for i in range(max_len):
    val_a = a[i] if i < len(a) else 'N/A'
wizard_code:
# Native Default Padding Zip
from itertools import zip_longest
paired = list(zip_longest(a, b, fillvalue='N/A'))
hinglish_explanation: Unequal length lists ko items truncate kiye bina join karne ke liye `zip_longest(a, b, fillvalue='N/A')` default filler attach karta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain itertools.zip_longest vs standard zip() with unequal lists.

title: Safe Dict Key Removal via dict.pop(key, None)
naive_code:
# Checking key existence before deleting
if 'internal_id' in record:
    val = record['internal_id']
    del record['internal_id']
wizard_code:
# Safe Key Extraction & Removal
val = record.pop('internal_id', None)
hinglish_explanation: `record.pop('key', None)` key ko remove karta hai aur value return karta hai, missing key hone par KeyError throw kiye bina None fallback deta hai!
ai_prompt: Show dict.pop() with fallback default values for clean dictionary mutation.

title: Initialize Key only if Missing via dict.setdefault()
naive_code:
# Checking key existence before assigning list default
if 'scores' not in user:
    user['scores'] = []
user['scores'].append(new_score)
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Auto-Set Accessor
user.setdefault('scores', []).append(new_score)
hinglish_explanation: `user.setdefault('scores', [])` key missing hone par list initialize karke reference return karta hai, 1 line me append karne ke liye!
ai_prompt: Compare dict.setdefault() vs collections.defaultdict usage.

title: Bulk Initialize Keys with Default Value via dict.fromkeys()
naive_code:
# Loop building dictionary with default value zero
counts = {col: 0 for col in feature_columns}
wizard_code:
# Native Factory Creator
counts = dict.fromkeys(feature_columns, 0)
hinglish_explanation: Columns list se initial default value (`0` ya `None`) dictionary taiyar karne ke liye `dict.fromkeys(cols, 0)` best shortcut hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate dict.fromkeys() with immutable vs mutable default values.

title: Check Disjoint Customer Cohorts via set.isdisjoint()
naive_code:
# Computing full intersection set to test overlap
if len(set_a & set_b) == 0: ...
wizard_code:
# O(min(N,M)) Short-Circuit Disjoint Check
if set_a.isdisjoint(set_b): ...
hinglish_explanation: Do customer lists me koi bhi common user hai ya nahi check karne ke liye `set_a.isdisjoint(set_b)` first match milte hi answer de deta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain set.isdisjoint() performance advantages over len(set_a & set_b) == 0.

title: Fast Unique List Preserving Order via dict.fromkeys()
naive_code:
# Converting to set removes duplicates but loses original order
unique_tags = list(set(raw_tags))
wizard_code:
# Fast Order-Preserving Unique List
unique_tags = list(dict.fromkeys(raw_tags))
hinglish_explanation: `set(tags)` duplicates remove karta hai par order destroy kar deta hai. `dict.fromkeys(tags)` insertion order preserve karke duplicates clear karta hai!
ai_prompt: Compare set deduplication vs dict.fromkeys deduplication for order preservation.

title: Safe Float Equality Check via math.isclose()
naive_code:
# Floating point precision bug (0.1 + 0.2 != 0.3 in IEEE 754)
if 0.1 + 0.2 == 0.3: print('Equal')
wizard_code:
# Safe Tolerance-Based Float Comparison
import math
if math.isclose(0.1 + 0.2, 0.3, rel_tol=1e-9):
    print('Equal')
hinglish_explanation: Float numbers math rounding errors ki wajah se direct `==` fail karta hai. `math.isclose(a, b)` tolerance-based safe equality check karta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain IEEE 754 floating point arithmetic issues and math.isclose() solution.

title: Modern File Path Chaining via pathlib.Path
naive_code:
# Legacy os.path joining strings
import os
file_path = os.path.join(os.getcwd(), 'data', 'raw_sales.csv')
wizard_code:
# Object-Oriented Clean Path Chaining
from pathlib import Path
file_path = Path.cwd() / 'data' / 'raw_sales.csv'
hinglish_explanation: Ugly `os.path.join` ki jagah modern Object-Oriented `Path.cwd() / 'data' / 'raw_sales.csv'` slash `/` operator use karo!
ai_prompt: Show pathlib.Path utilities like Path.read_text(), Path.write_text(), and Path.glob().

title: Recursive File Globbing via pathlib.Path.rglob()
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Recursive Glob Generator
from pathlib import Path
csv_files = list(Path('.').rglob('*.csv'))
hinglish_explanation: Subdirectories ke andar saare `.csv` files recursively scan karne ke liye `os.walk` ki jagah `Path('.').rglob('*.csv')` 1-liner use karo!
ai_prompt: Compare os.walk() vs pathlib.Path.rglob() for scanning dataset directory trees.

title: Direct File Read & Write via Path.read_text() & write_text()
naive_code:
# Multi-line open context manager for reading text file
with open('data.json', 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    content = f.read()
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Text File Reader
from pathlib import Path
content = Path('data.json').read_text(encoding='utf-8')
hinglish_explanation: Entire text file ko single line me read ya write karne ke liye `Path('file.txt').read_text()` cleanest 1-liner function hai!
ai_prompt: Show Path.read_text() and Path.write_text() with encoding options.

title: Extract File Extension & Base Name via Path.stem & Path.suffix
naive_code:
# Index string slicing to separate file name and extension
name, ext = filename.rsplit('.', 1)
wizard_code:
# Native Path Attribute Decomposition
from pathlib import Path
p = Path('/data/sales_report.v2.csv')
base_name = p.stem   # 'sales_report.v2'
extension = p.suffix  # '.csv'
hinglish_explanation: Path string me se extension (`.csv`) aur base name (`sales_report`) extract karne ke liye `path.stem` aur `path.suffix` use karo!
ai_prompt: Explain pathlib attributes: stem, suffix, parent, and name.

title: Auto-Clean Scratch Directories via tempfile.TemporaryDirectory
naive_code:
# Manually creating temp folder and deleting in finally block
temp_folder = '/tmp/processing'
os.mkdir(temp_folder)
# ... cleanup code
wizard_code:
# Self-Cleaning Scratch Directory
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path
with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as temp_dir:
    scratch_file = Path(temp_dir) / 'scratch.csv'
    scratch_file.write_text('id,score\n1,100')
hinglish_explanation: Temporary processing files create karne ke liye `with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory():` block exit hone par folder auto-wipe kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Show tempfile.TemporaryDirectory usage for ETL scratch file processing.

title: Convert CamelCase Keys to snake_case via Regular Expressions
naive_code:
# Loop checking uppercase letters to insert underscores
new_key = ''
for char in key: ...
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Regex Key Converter
import re
snake_key = re.sub(r'(?<!^)(?=[A-Z])', '_', camel_key).lower()
hinglish_explanation: API JSON CamelCase keys (`userId`, `orderAmount`) ko Pythonic `snake_case` me convert karne ke liye `re.sub(r'(?<!^)(?=[A-Z])', '_', s).lower()` use karo!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate regex substitution for converting CamelCase to snake_case.

title: Human-Readable Title Capitalization via string.capwords
naive_code:
# Standard str.title() capitalizes characters after apostrophes incorrectly
clean = "they're learning".title() # Outputs: "They'Re Learning"
wizard_code:
# Smart Word-Based Title Capitalizer
import string
clean = string.capwords("they're learning") # Outputs: "They're Learning"
hinglish_explanation: `str.title()` apostrophes ke baad wale letters ko galat capitalize karta hai. `string.capwords()` clean spaces based title casing karta hai!
ai_prompt: Compare str.title() vs string.capwords() with edge cases.

title: Extract Clean Words from Dirty Text via re.findall
naive_code:
# String split missing punctuation cleanup
words = text.split()
wizard_code:
# Exact Regex Word Tokenizer
import re
clean_words = re.findall(r'\w+', text)
hinglish_explanation: Punctuation marks ignore karke pure text me se clean words extract karne ke liye `re.findall(r'\w+', text)` best word tokenizer hai!
ai_prompt: Show re.findall() for word tokenization and text analysis.

title: Format Large Currency Numbers with Commas via F-Strings
naive_code:
# Manual loop or string slicing inserting commas
formatted = f'{num:,}'
wizard_code:
# Native Thousand Separator Formatting
formatted = f'{1000000000:,}'  # Outputs: '1,000,000,000'
hinglish_explanation: Large numbers ko human-readable comma separator format me print karne ke liye F-String me `:`, specifier (`f'{val:,}'`) apply karo!
ai_prompt: Show F-String number formatting specifiers for currency, percentages, and floats.

title: Get Key with Maximum Value via max(d, key=d.get)
naive_code:
# Full sort of dictionary items tuple list
top_key = sorted(counts.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)[0][0]
wizard_code:
# Direct 1-Liner Highest Value Key Extractor
top_key = max(counts, key=counts.get)
hinglish_explanation: Dictionary me sabse highest value wali Key find karne ke liye `max(dict, key=dict.get)` 1-liner call karo!
ai_prompt: Show max() and min() on Python dictionaries by key vs value.

title: Filter Missing Data with List Comprehensions
wizard_code:
# Compact Truthy List Comprehension Filter
cleaned = [x for x in raw_records if x]
hinglish_explanation: List me se empty strings, None, aur zero values filter karne ke liye manual loop mat lagao. `[x for x in list if x]` implicit truthiness use karke clean filter karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show list comprehensions with multiple conditional filters for data cleaning.

title: Scale Numeric Data to Range 0-1 Vectorwise
naive_code:
# Manual loop dividing min-max difference
scaled = []
min_v, max_v = min(vals), max(vals)
diff = max_v - min_v
for v in vals:
    scaled.append((v - min_v) / diff)
wizard_code:
# List Comprehension Min-Max Scaler
min_v, max_v = min(vals), max(vals)
diff = (max_v - min_v) or 1  # Avoid division by zero
scaled = [(v - min_v) / diff for v in vals]
hinglish_explanation: Features normalization (Min-Max Scaling) ko pure Python list level par perform karne ke liye simple range computation aur division logic list comprehension me apply karo!
ai_prompt: Explain Min-Max normalization implementation in Python list comprehensions.

title: Detect Outliers using Z-Score in Pure Python
naive_code:
# Nested loops computing mean and standard deviation manually
# ... 15 lines of code
wizard_code:
# Math-Based Z-Score Outlier Filter
import statistics
mean_v = statistics.mean(data)
stdev_v = statistics.stdev(data) or 1
outliers = [x for x in data if abs(x - mean_v) / stdev_v > 3]
hinglish_explanation: Data distribution se extreme values (outliers) filter karne ke liye statistics library ke `mean` aur `stdev` calculations use karke list comprehension filter apply karo!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate Z-Score outlier detection logic in pure Python statistics module.

title: Compute Median / Percentiles using statistics.quantiles()
naive_code:
# Sorting list manually and dividing index to find quartiles
# ... complex math index checks
wizard_code:
# Native Quantile Divider (Python 3.8+)
import statistics
quartiles = statistics.quantiles(data, n=4)
hinglish_explanation: Dataset ko 4 equal parts (Quartiles) me cut karne ke liye index-slicing math mat calculate karo. `statistics.quantiles(data, n=4)` direct cutoff thresholds list deta hai!
ai_prompt: Show statistics.quantiles() usage for dividing distributions into deciles (n=10).

title: Conditional Replacements using 1-Liner Ternary Operators
wizard_code:
# Ternary Expression inside Comprehension
cleaned = [x if x >= 0 else 0 for x in data]
hinglish_explanation: List elements replacement (e.g. negative numbers threshold capping) inline ternary check `[x if x >= 0 else 0]` se 1 line me execute ho jata hai!
ai_prompt: Show inline ternary operators inside Python list comprehensions.

title: Un-nest Dictionary Keys using Dictionary Merging
wizard_code:
# Dictionary Star Unpacking Merge
flat = {'id': d['id'], **d['info']}
hinglish_explanation: Nested JSON response key payloads ('info') ko direct parent namespace level par un-nest (flatten) karne ke liye dynamic star double unpacking `{**parent, **nested}` apply karo!
ai_prompt: Show how to flatten nested dictionaries using dictionary star unpacking.

title: Check Duplicate Items using set() Comparison
naive_code:
# Loop tracking elements in list to find if duplicate exists
has_duplicates = False
seen = set()
for x in data:
    if x in seen: has_duplicates = True; break
    seen.add(x)
wizard_code:
# 1-Liner Unique Set Length Check
has_duplicates = len(data) != len(set(data))
hinglish_explanation: Puri data array me koi duplicate value exist karti hai ya nahi, set length check `len(data) != len(set(data))` se direct constant time validation deta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain set complexity checks for detecting duplicates in list data.

title: Chunk Large Lists into Batches using Generators
naive_code:
# Creating multiple list slices in memory (bloats memory)
batches = [data[i:i+100] for i in range(0, len(data), 100)]
hinglish_explanation: Millions of rows ko batch pipelines (e.g. database inserts) me chunks me split karne ke liye `yield` slices use karo, jo stream basis par RAM utilization minimal rakhta hai!
ai_prompt: Show Python generator implementations for batch processing massive CSV streams.

title: Accumulate Running Metrics with itertools.accumulate()
naive_code:
# Loop building running max list manually
curr_max = data[0]
running_max = []
for x in data:
    curr_max = max(curr_max, x)
    running_max.append(curr_max)
wizard_code:
# Native Cumulative Metric Streamer
from itertools import accumulate
running_max = list(accumulate(data, max))
hinglish_explanation: Running totals ke alawa Running Max ya Running Min timeline track karne ke liye `accumulate(data, max)` inline parameter utility utilize karo!
ai_prompt: Show itertools.accumulate with custom comparison operators for time-series metrics.

title: Generate Combinations Matrix via itertools.combinations()
wizard_code:
# Unique Math Combination Generator
from itertools import combinations
pairs = list(combinations(items, 2))
hinglish_explanation: Features columns, tags, ya target sets ke beech uniquely paired combinations generate karne ke liye `combinations(items, 2)` C-level math optimization provide karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show how itertools.combinations computes correlation pair matrices in pure Python.

title: Slice Data Streams without Conversion via itertools.islice()
naive_code:
# Loading huge iterator stream to list to slice top 5 items
first_5 = list(large_stream)[:5]
wizard_code:
# O(1) Memory Stream Slicer
from itertools import islice
first_5 = list(islice(large_stream, 5))
hinglish_explanation: Large generator stream me se top rows slice karne ke liye generator elements list me convert mat karo; `islice(stream, 5)` zero RAM duplication ensures karta hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate islice usage for parsing header lines from huge log files.

title: Format Percentage Ratios cleanly via F-Strings
naive_code:
# Manual calculation and string rounding string formatting
pct = str(round((numerator / denominator) * 100, 2)) + '%'
wizard_code:
# Native Precision Percentage Formatter
pct = f'{numerator / denominator:.2%}'  # Outputs: '42.50%'
hinglish_explanation: Percentage metrics format karne ke liye math calculations aur manually `%` append karne se bacho. F-String format specifier `:.2%` automatic multiply aur precision handle karta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain precision and type specs in Python F-String float representations.

title: Find Binary Lookup Placement Index via bisect.bisect()
naive_code:
# Loop checking thresholds sequentially to assign numeric grades
grade = 'F'
for score, g in threshold_ranges:
    if val >= score: grade = g
wizard_code:
# O(log N) Quantile Bucket Lookup
import bisect
breakpoints = [60, 70, 80, 90]
grades = ['F', 'D', 'C', 'B', 'A']
idx = bisect.bisect(breakpoints, score)
grade = grades[idx]
hinglish_explanation: Sorted numeric boundaries (grades, income slabs) me target score ki bracket classification bucket locate karne ke liye `bisect` binary lookup O(log N) run karo!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate bisect.bisect() for implementing custom range-based mapping functions.

title: Track Execution Memory Peak using resource.getrusage()
naive_code:
# Guessing RAM footprint spike during pipeline execution
print('Done processing')
wizard_code:
# Peak Resident Set Size Memory Query
import resource
peak_kb = resource.getrusage(resource.RUSAGE_SELF).ru_maxrss
# Note: ru_maxrss outputs in kilobytes on Linux, bytes on macOS
hinglish_explanation: System level par data pipeline runs ke waqt maximum peak memory usage (Peak RAM RSS spike) inspect karne ke liye `resource.getrusage` direct hardware query report karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show getrusage resource tracking context manager for timing and memory performance analytics.

title: Deduplicate and Retain Order on Lists of Dictionaries
naive_code:
# Complex list scans tracking unique keys manually
seen = set()
unique_records = []
for r in records:
    if r['email'] not in seen:
        seen.add(r['email'])
        unique_records.append(r)
wizard_code:
# Unique Dictionary List Comprehension
seen = set()
unique_records = [r for r in records if not (r['email'] in seen or seen.add(r['email']))]
hinglish_explanation: List of dicts me specific key (e.g. Email) ki uniqueness enforce karte huye duplicate records 1-liner comprehension set optimization tricks se drop karo!
ai_prompt: Write a clean order-preserving list of dictionaries deduplication function in Python.

title: Check Common Keys Overlap between Dictionaries

common_keys = list(dict1.keys() & dict2.keys())

title: Check all Elements Equality in List via set.pop() Check

# 1-Liner Uniformity Validation Check

title: Clean Multi-Attribute Sorting via tuple sorting
naive_code:
# Sorting twice sequentially to priority rank rows
# ... unstable
wizard_code:
# Tuple-Based Prioritized Single Pass Sort
# Sorts by Spend descending (negative spend), then by Age ascending
records.sort(key=lambda r: (-r['spend'], r['age']))
hinglish_explanation: Do criteria par entries sort karne ke liye priority tuple key return karo (`lambda r: (-spend, age)`). Highest spend values pehle sorted hongi, same spend par age index level sort ensure hoga!
ai_prompt: Show multi-criteria list sorting using sign inversion for numeric priority columns.

title: Remove Accented Unicode Characters dynamically
naive_code:
# Hardcoded replacements of special letters like à, é, ü
clean = text.replace('é', 'e').replace('ü', 'u')
wizard_code:
# NFKD Decomposition accent stripper
import unicodedata
clean = ''.join(c for c in unicodedata.normalize('NFD', text) if unicodedata.category(c) != 'Mn')
hinglish_explanation: International text database search indexing ke liye accented names (e.g. `Müller` -> `Muller`) clean karne ke liye NFKD decomposition character categorization utilize karo!
ai_prompt: Show unicodedata char categorization for normalization of text files.

title: Count Exact Word Occurrences via re.findall() Word Boundaries
naive_code:
# Standard str.count() matches substring fractions (e.g. 'cat' in 'category')
count = text.lower().count('cat')
wizard_code:
# Exact Word Boundary Match Count
import re
count = len(re.findall(r'\bcat\b', text.lower()))
hinglish_explanation: `str.count('cat')` string 'category' ke segment ko count kar leta hai. Exact individual word match trace karne ke liye regular expression word boundary boundary `\b` check karo!
ai_prompt: Show re.findall with word boundaries vs standard string counts in Python.

title: Compact File Copy Operations via shutil.copyfileobj()
naive_code:
# Loading entire heavy file to memory buffer before writing copy
with open(src, 'r') as r:
    data = r.read()
with open(dst, 'w') as w:
    w.write(data)
wizard_code:
# Stream-Based Buffered Chunk Copy
import shutil
with open(src, 'rb') as r, open(dst, 'wb') as w:
    shutil.copyfileobj(r, w, length=16*1024)
hinglish_explanation: GB-sized raw dataset files duplicate karte waqt memory spikes avoid karne ke liye `shutil.copyfileobj` chunk buffer copy utility execution chalao!
ai_prompt: Explain memory-friendly file copy operations using python shutil library.

title: Extract URL Query Parameters via urllib.parse.parse_qs()
naive_code:
# Manual split loops parsing string parameters
params = {x.split('=')[0]: x.split('=')[1] for x in url.split('?')[1].split('&')}
wizard_code:
# Native Query URL string parser
from urllib.parse import parse_qs, urlparse
query_params = parse_qs(urlparse(url).query)
hinglish_explanation: Log file click URLs (`?campaign_id=123&source=web`) ke dynamic parameters parse karne ke liye `urllib.parse.parse_qs` standard parser use karo!
ai_prompt: Show how urllib.parse constructs clean key-value parameter maps from log URLs.

title: Group List Elements by custom lambda key via itertools.groupby
naive_code:
# Nested grouping loops or list comprehensions sorting keys

wizard_code:
# C-speed Consecutive Group Aggregation
from itertools import groupby
# Note: data must be sorted by key first
sorted_data = sorted(records, key=lambda x: x['dept'])
groups = {k: list(g) for k, g in groupby(sorted_data, key=lambda x: x['dept'])}
hinglish_explanation: Data list ko consecutive blocks me categorise group aggregated lookup dict generate karne ke liye `itertools.groupby()` use karo!

title: Calculate Exact Monotonic Elapsed Timing with time.monotonic()
naive_code:
# Using time.time() which can warp backwards if system sync updates clock

wizard_code:
# Monotonic Non-Warping Hardware clock timer
t0 = time.monotonic()
run_pipeline()
elapsed = time.monotonic() - t0
hinglish_explanation: Long execution running queries time metric capture karte waqt NTP server system time clock sync drift safe calculations ke liye `time.monotonic()` use karo!

title: Extract specific capture group matches using re.search()
naive_code:

val = log[idx+3:idx+8]
wizard_code:
# Match Group Capture Pattern Extraction
import re
if (match := re.search(r'ID:([A-Z0-9]{5})', log)):
    val = match.group(1)
hinglish_explanation: Logs key raw text entries se structured ID patterns (`ID:A123B`) cleanly capture group extract karne ke liye `re.search()` write karo!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate capture groups extraction in python re.search and re.match.

title: Read Fixed Byte Chunks from Files to optimize RAM
naive_code:
# Reading massive raw text dump directly to single RAM string
with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
    data = f.read()
wizard_code:
# Chunk-by-Chunk Buffered Processing Loop
with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
    while (chunk := f.read(4096)):
        process_chunk(chunk)
hinglish_explanation: Gigabyte-sized data logs ya CSVs read karte waqt memory overflow protection ke liye raw read buffer chunk (`f.read(4096)`) size constrain loop chalao!
ai_prompt: Show how to read files in fixed byte size chunks inside python while loops.

title: Format Datetime into Standard ISO 8601 Strings via isoformat()

title: Compare Sets containment quickly via superset operator
